title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following the questioning of Linda Marshall, Colonel Weston and Hercule Poirot discuss her potential involvement. Poirot and Weston conclude that Christine Redfern is cleared of suspicion; Poirot believes she lacks the impulsive nature and physical capability required for the murder, and Inspector Corrigan notes that Dr. Nesden described the killer as having large, strong hands, whereas Christine has small, delicate hands.

The investigation then shifts to Patrick Redfern. Under questioning by Weston and Poirot, Patrick admits he has been "madly in love" with the deceased, Elena Marshall, for three months. He confesses to meeting her several times, noting that his wife, Christine, was unaware of the extent of the affair. Patrick claims that while he was infatuated with Elena, he truly loves his wife. Colonel Weston suggests that the affair provides a motive for murder, specifically proposing that Elena's husband, Kenneth Marshall, might have killed her upon discovering the relationship. Patrick denies this possibility, describing Marshall as a quiet person.

Patrick reveals that he and Elena usually met on the beach in the morning to go out on a raft, but she did not show up that morning. He mentions that they sometimes met in the afternoon at Gull Bay to avoid crowds. Regarding other locations, Patrick notes that they avoided Fairy Cove in the mornings because it is too busy with people taking boats out, though they sometimes walked on the island after dinner. Weston concludes the session by noting that Patrick has provided no information regarding why Elena went to Fairy Cove that morning.
